response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that is commonly found in many products and are not related to water or underwater environments  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot consume peanuts and are not affected by the physical properties of water  
allergies are based on the immune system and not on the presence of water in the environment  
the immune system of someone allergic to peanuts reacts to the proteins in peanuts and not to water  
the reaction to peanuts is a biological response and not a reaction to the physical state of water  
the body of someone allergic to peanuts is not affected by the presence of water in the environment  

Sec. 2  
the physical properties of water do not affect the immune system or the proteins in peanuts  
the immune system does not recognize water as a threat and does not react to it  
the allergic reaction is based on the presence of specific proteins in peanuts and not on the environment  
the body of someone allergic to peanuts is not affected by the presence of water in the environment  
the physical state of water does not trigger an allergic reaction and does not affect the immune system
